channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: Erase at the Core: Representation Unlearning for Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Erase at the Core (EC), a framework to enforce forgetting across intermediate network representations rather than only at the classifier logits.
  • Applies multi-layer contrastive unlearning on the forget set combined with deeply supervised retention (cross-entropy) on the retain set via auxiliary modules attached to intermediate layers.
  • Shows EC reduces representational similarity to the original model across layers, is model-agnostic, and can be plugged into existing unlearning methods while maintaining retain-set performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jaewon Lee', 'Yongwoo Kim', 'Donghyun Kim']
  • Tags: ['machine-unlearning', 'privacy-preserving-ML', 'representation-unlearning', 'contrastive-learning', 'defense']

item.title: Towards Privacy-Guaranteed Label Unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning: Few-Shot Forgetting without Disclosure
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the first method for label unlearning specifically in Vertical Federated Learning (VFL), addressing labels as both inputs and sensitive information.
  • Uses representation-level manifold mixup to synthesize embeddings for unlearned and retained samples, providing richer signals for subsequent gradient-based label forgetting.
  • Introduces a recovery-phase optimization to restore performance on retained data while maintaining computational efficiency.
  • Validates effectiveness and scalability across diverse datasets (MNIST, CIFAR-10/100, ModelNet, medical imaging, Yahoo Answers) and releases code.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hanlin Gu', 'Hong Xi Tae', 'Lixin Fan', 'Chee Seng Chan']
  • Tags: ['vertical-federated-learning', 'label-unlearning', 'privacy-preserving', 'unlearning-defense', 'representation-mixup']

item.title: Self-Supervised AI-Generated Image Detection: A Camera Metadata Perspective
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a self-supervised detector for AI-generated images that learns photographic features from EXIF camera metadata by classifying categorical EXIF tags and pairwise-ranking ordinal/continuous EXIF tags.
  • Uses the learned EXIF-induced features for one-class detection via a Gaussian mixture model (flagging low-likelihood images as AI-generated) and for binary detection by regularizing a classifier operating on high-frequency residuals from spatially scrambled patches.
  • Demonstrates strong cross-model generalization and robustness to common benign perturbations across multiple generative models; code and models are released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nan Zhong', 'Mian Zou', 'Yiran Xu', 'Zhenxing Qian', 'Xinpeng Zhang', 'Baoyuan Wu', 'Kede Ma']
  • Tags: ['AI-generated image detection', 'multimedia forensics', 'self-supervised learning', 'EXIF metadata', 'robustness/generalization']

item.title: Veritas: Generalizable Deepfake Detection via Pattern-Aware Reasoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces HydraFake, a hierarchical dataset simulating real-world deepfake challenges (diverse forgery techniques, in-the-wild images, and OOD evaluation protocols).
  • Proposes Veritas, an MLLM-based deepfake detector using 'pattern-aware reasoning' (planning and self-reflection) to emulate forensic workflows and provide transparent detections.
  • Presents a two-stage training pipeline to inject deepfake reasoning into MLLMs and reports strong improvements in out-of-distribution generalization (unseen forgeries and domains) compared to prior detectors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hao Tan', 'Jun Lan', 'Zichang Tan', 'Ajian Liu', 'Chuanbiao Song', 'Senyuan Shi', 'Huijia Zhu', 'Weiqiang Wang', 'Jun Wan', 'Zhen Lei']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-detection', 'dataset', 'robustness', 'multimodal-llm', 'forensic-reasoning']

item.title: Concept-based Adversarial Attack: a Probabilistic Perspective
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a probabilistic, concept-based adversarial attack that operates on distributions representing a concept rather than single-image perturbations.
  • Generates diverse adversarial samples by sampling from a concept-based adversarial distribution while preserving the underlying concept (pose, viewpoint, background variations).
  • Claims theoretical consistency with traditional adversarial attacks and shows empirical improvements in diversity and attack efficiency while maintaining concept identity.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Andi Zhang', 'Xuan Ding', 'Steven McDonagh', 'Samuel Kaski']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'concept-based attack', 'probabilistic modeling', 'image robustness', 'evasion']

item.title: GenVidBench: A 6-Million Benchmark for AI-Generated Video Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces GenVidBench, a large-scale dataset for AI-generated video detection containing 6.78 million videos — currently the largest for this task.
  • Dataset design emphasizes cross-source and cross-generator splits to reduce content bias and improve generalization between training and test sets.
  • Includes outputs from 11 state-of-the-art video generators to cover recent generative advances and increase diversity of synthetic artifacts.
  • Provides extensive experiments with advanced video classification models and releases datasets and code for the community.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhenliang Ni', 'Qiangyu Yan', 'Mouxiao Huang', 'Tianning Yuan', 'Yehui Tang', 'Hailin Hu', 'Xinghao Chen', 'Yunhe Wang']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'dataset/benchmark', 'video forensics', 'generative media detection']

item.title: Leveraging large multimodal models for audio-video deepfake detection: a pilot study
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AV-LMMDetect, a supervised fine-tuned large multimodal model (based on Qwen 2.5 Omni) for audio-visual deepfake detection formulated as a prompted yes/no classification.
  • Training pipeline: lightweight LoRA alignment followed by full fine-tuning of audio-visual encoders.
  • Evaluated on FakeAVCeleb and Mavos-DD, matching or surpassing prior methods and achieving a new state of the art on Mavos-DD.
  • Focus is on building a scalable, generalizable multimodal defense (deepfake detection) leveraging large multimodal models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Songjun Cao (Tencent YouTu Lab', 'China)', 'Yuqi Li (Tencent YouTu Lab', 'China', 'Fudan University', 'China)', 'Yunpeng Luo (Tencent YouTu Lab', 'China)', 'Jianjun Yin (Fudan University', 'China)', 'Long Ma (Tencent YouTu Lab', 'China)']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'multimodal LMM', 'audio-visual', 'LoRA/fine-tuning', 'defense/security']

item.title: Look Carefully: Adaptive Visual Reinforcements in Multimodal Large Language Models for Hallucination Mitigation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AIR (Adaptive Visual Reinforcement), a training-free framework to reduce hallucinations in multimodal large language models by selectively reinforcing visual tokens during decoding.
  • Two components: prototype-based token reduction to compress visual tokens, and OT-guided patch reinforcement to align and inject the most consistent image patches into feed-forward layers.
  • Claims substantial reduction in hallucination across representative MLLMs while preserving general capabilities, with no additional training or large inference latency overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Zhu', 'Kesen Zhao', 'Liang Yi', 'Shuo Wang', 'Zhicai Wang', 'Beier Zhu', 'Hanwang Zhang']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'multimodal robustness', 'defense mechanism', 'visual token selection']

item.title: NAU-QMUL: Utilizing BERT and CLIP for Multi-modal AI-Generated Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a multi-modal, multi-task detector that uses pre-trained BERT (text) and CLIP (image) encoders with cross-modal feature fusion to detect AI-generated images and attribute their source model.
  • Uses a tailored multi-task loss and pseudo-labeling data augmentation to expand training data with high-confidence samples.
  • Evaluated in the CT2: AI-Generated Image Detection competition, achieving 5th place in both Task A (binary detection) and Task B (model attribution) with F1 scores of 83.16% and 48.88%, respectively.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaoyu Guo', 'Arkaitz Zubiaga']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'multimodal detection', 'model attribution', 'pseudo-labeling', 'CLIP/BERT']

item.title: Suppressing Prior-Comparison Hallucinations in Radiology Report Generation via Semantically Decoupled Latent Steering
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Semantically Decoupled Latent Steering (SDLS), a training-free, inference-time control method to suppress prior-comparison (historical) hallucinations in radiology report generation.
  • Constructs a semantic-free intervention vector via LLM-driven semantic decomposition followed by QR-based orthogonalization to isolate the "historical comparison" axis and avoid semantic entanglement common in PCA/activation steering.
  • Validates SDLS on BiomedGPT with experiments on MIMIC-CXR and zero-shot transfer to CheXpert Plus and IU-Xray, showing reduced historical hallucination probability (FilBERT 0.2373 → 0.1889) and improved clinical fidelity (CheXpert macro-F1 0.2242 → 0.3208).
  • Claims maintenance of clinical narrative structure while overcoming the trade-off between hallucination suppression and clinical accuracy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ao Li', 'Rui Liu', 'Mingjie Li', 'Sheng Liu', 'Lei Wang', 'Xiaodan Liang', 'Lina Yao', 'Xiaojun Chang', 'Lei Xing']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'inference-time defense', 'vision-language models', 'medical AI safety']

item.title: All in One: Unifying Deepfake Detection, Tampering Localization, and Source Tracing with a Robust Landmark-Identity Watermark
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LIDMark, a 152-dimensional landmark-identity watermark that embeds facial landmarks and a unique source identifier to enable unified deepfake detection, tampering localization, and source tracing.
  • Introduces a Factorized-Head Decoder (FHD) that splits backbone features into regression (landmark reconstruction) and classification (source ID decoding) heads to robustly recover the watermark under distortion or tampering.
  • Uses an intrinsic-extrinsic consistency check from the regression head for detection/localization and the classification head for reliable source attribution; reports extensive experiments demonstrating robustness and imperceptibility.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junjiang Wu', 'Liejun Wang', 'Zhiqing Guo']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'proactive watermarking', 'tampering localization', 'source attribution', 'robustness']

item.title: On the Effectiveness of Membership Inference in Targeted Data Extraction from Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Integrates multiple membership inference attack (MIA) techniques into a targeted training-data extraction pipeline for LLMs.
  • Systematically benchmarks the effectiveness of these MIAs when used as part of a data-extraction workflow.
  • Compares integrated extraction+MIA performance against results from conventional standalone MIA benchmarks.
  • Evaluates the practical utility of MIAs for real-world targeted data extraction and privacy leakage from LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ali Al Sahili', 'Ali Chehab', 'Razane Tajeddine']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'data-extraction', 'model-privacy', 'LLM-memorization', 'privacy-benchmarking']

item.title: Open Rubric System: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with Pairwise Adaptive Rubric
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Open Rubric System (OpenRS), a rubric-based LLM-as-judge framework that uses Pairwise Adaptive Meta-Rubrics (PAMR) to generate criterion-wise, pairwise comparisons between candidate responses rather than compressing preferences into a single scalar.
  • Introduces Pointwise Verifiable Rubrics (PVRs) that provide hard-constraint guardrails and verifiable reward components for objective sub-tasks to reduce reward hacking and brittleness.
  • Uses a two-level meta-rubric refinement pipeline (automated evolutionary refinement for general principles + reproducible human-in-the-loop for domain principles) to maintain consistent, inspectable alignment principles.
  • Demonstrates application of OpenRS as reward supervision in pairwise RL training to improve robustness and discriminability in open-ended alignment tasks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruipeng Jia', 'Yunyi Yang', 'Yuxin Wu', 'Yongbo Gai', 'Siyuan Tao', 'Mengyu Zhou', 'Jianhe Lin', 'Xiaoxi Jiang', 'Guanjun Jiang']
  • Tags: ['alignment', 'reward_hacking', 'guardrails', 'robustness', 'RL_training']

item.title: Interpreting Transformers Through Attention Head Intervention
item.description:
Summary

  • Surveys the emergence of attention head intervention as a causal method for mechanistic interpretability of transformers, marking a shift from visualization to direct intervention.
  • Synthesizes empirical findings about what attention-head interventions reveal and discusses limitations and complications for interpretation.
  • Highlights practical outcomes where mechanistic understanding enables targeted control of model behavior, including suppression of toxic outputs and manipulation of semantic content.
  • Positions attention-head intervention as a tool with concrete AI-safety implications (defensive/control mechanisms) rather than focusing on adversarial attacks or privacy breaches.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mason Kadem', 'Rong Zheng']
  • Tags: ['mechanistic-interpretability', 'attention-head-intervention', 'AI-safety', 'defense/control']

item.title: Tracing and Reversing Edits in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the tasks of tracing (identifying the edited object/entity) and reversing (undoing) knowledge edits in LLMs to defend against malicious or unauthorized model modifications.
  • Presents a method to infer the edited entity solely from modified model weights with up to ~99% accuracy, without access to the editing prompt or semantically similar prompts.
  • Proposes a training-free reversal technique that restores up to ~94% of edits and can also be used to distinguish edited from unedited weights.
  • Demonstrates feasibility of weight-space forensics and mitigation, opening directions for detecting and undoing adversarial knowledge injections.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Paul Youssef', 'Zhixue Zhao', 'Christin Seifert', 'J\\"org Schl\\"otterer']
  • Tags: ['knowledge editing', 'model forensics', 'defense/mitigation', 'adversarial model tampering']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Jailbreak Foundry (JBF), a system that translates jailbreak papers into runnable attack modules via a multi-agent workflow.
  • Consists of JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluations) to enable reproducible attack execution and scoring.
  • Reproduced 30 jailbreak attacks with high fidelity (mean ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduced per-attack implementation code by ~50%, and achieved an 82.5% mean reused-code ratio.
  • Runs a standardized AdvBench evaluation of the 30 attacks across 10 victim models using a consistent GPT-4o judge, enabling living benchmarks that track evolving vulnerabilities.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-attacks', 'reproducibility', 'red-teaming', 'benchmarking']

item.title: NAU-QMUL: Utilizing BERT and CLIP for Multi-modal AI-Generated Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a multi-modal, multi-task detector that uses pre-trained BERT (text) and CLIP (image) encoders with cross-modal feature fusion to detect AI-generated images and attribute their source model.
  • Uses a tailored multi-task loss and pseudo-labeling data augmentation to expand training data with high-confidence samples.
  • Evaluated in the CT2: AI-Generated Image Detection competition, achieving 5th place in both Task A (binary detection) and Task B (model attribution) with F1 scores of 83.16% and 48.88%, respectively.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaoyu Guo', 'Arkaitz Zubiaga']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'multimodal detection', 'model attribution', 'pseudo-labeling', 'CLIP/BERT']

item.title: Controllable Reasoning Models Are Private Thinkers
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes training reasoning models to follow instructions not only for final answers but also for internal reasoning traces, to reduce unintended leakage of sensitive data.
  • Introduces a new instruction-following dataset with explicit restrictions on reasoning traces and a generation strategy that decouples reasoning and answer generation via separate LoRA adapters.
  • Evaluates across six models (1.7B–14B) on two instruction-following benchmarks and two privacy benchmarks, reporting up to +51.9 percentage points on privacy metrics but noting trade-offs with task utility.
  • Provides code and data for reproducibility, demonstrating a concrete defense approach against information leakage from model reasoning traces.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haritz Puerto', 'Haonan Li', 'Xudong Han', 'Timothy Baldwin', 'Iryna Gurevych']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving LLMs', 'reasoning-trace control', 'data-leakage prevention', 'adapter/LoRA defenses']

item.title: LFQA-HP-1M: A Large-Scale Human Preference Dataset for Long-Form Question Answering
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces LFQA-HP-1M: a large-scale dataset of 1.3M human pairwise preference annotations for long-form question answering with nine proposed rubrics for answer quality.
  • Shows that simple linear models based on rubric features match state-of-the-art LLM evaluators and provides analysis of evaluator behaviors (transitivity, positional bias, verbosity bias).
  • Demonstrates that LLM evaluators are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations and characterizes evaluator failure modes, suggesting potential security/robustness concerns for automated evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rafid Ishrak Jahan', 'Fahmid Shahriar Iqbal', 'Sagnik Ray Choudhury']
  • Tags: ['dataset', 'LLM evaluator', 'adversarial robustness', 'evaluation/bias analysis', 'long-form QA']

item.title: CiteAudit: You Cited It, But Did You Read It? A Benchmark for Verifying Scientific References in the LLM Era
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the first comprehensive benchmark and human-validated dataset for detecting hallucinated/fabricated citations in scientific writing.
  • Proposes a multi-agent verification pipeline that decomposes citation checking into claim extraction, evidence retrieval, passage matching, reasoning, and calibrated judgment.
  • Defines unified metrics for citation faithfulness and evidence alignment and provides scalable infrastructure for auditing references.
  • Evaluates state-of-the-art LLMs, demonstrating substantial citation errors and showing the proposed framework outperforms prior methods in accuracy and interpretability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhengqing Yuan', 'Kaiwen Shi', 'Zheyuan Zhang', 'Lichao Sun', 'Nitesh V. Chawla', 'Yanfang Ye']
  • Tags: ['citation verification', 'hallucination detection', 'LLM safety', 'benchmark', 'information integrity']

item.title: Discrete Optimal Transport and Voice Conversion
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes using discrete optimal transport with barycentric projection to align audio embeddings for high-quality voice conversion.
  • Performs ablations on number of embeddings and compares to kNN averaging and prior OT results.
  • Shows that applying discrete OT as a post-processing step to synthetic audio can cause it to be misclassified as real, constituting a novel adversarial attack against synthetic/spoof detectors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anton Selitskiy', 'Maitreya Kocharekar']
  • Tags: ['audio adversarial attack', 'voice conversion', 'optimal transport', 'spoofing/detection evasion']

item.title: Manifold of Failure: Behavioral Attraction Basins in Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a framework to map the 'Manifold of Failure' in LLMs by treating vulnerability discovery as a quality-diversity search problem and using MAP-Elites to produce global, interpretable maps of failure regions (behavioral attraction basins).
  • Proposes a metric, Alignment Deviation, to guide search toward behaviors that diverge from intended alignment, and demonstrates the method across three LLMs (Llama-3-8B, GPT-OSS-20B, GPT-5-Mini).
  • Reports quantitative results: up to 63% behavioral coverage, discovery of up to 370 distinct vulnerability niches, and model-specific topological signatures (plateaus, fragmented basins, robustness ceilings).
  • Claims the approach reveals continuous topology of unsafe regions and provides information that existing attack methods (GCG, PAIR, TAP) do not, shifting focus from discrete failures to structural understanding of vulnerabilities.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sarthak Munshi', 'Manish Bhatt', 'Vineeth Sai Narajala', 'Idan Habler', 'Ammar Al-Kahfah', 'Ken Huang', 'Blake Gatto']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'red-teaming', 'vulnerability-mapping', 'quality-diversity', 'alignment-deviation']

item.title: To Deceive is to Teach? Forging Perceptual Robustness via Adversarial Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AOT-SFT, a large-scale adversarial dataset to improve perceptual robustness of multimodal LLMs.
  • Introduces AOT (Adversarial Opponent Training), a self-play framework where an image-editing Attacker generates challenging perturbations and a Defender MLLM adapts via co-evolution.
  • Shows that training with AOT improves Defender robustness to visually complex scenes and reduces hallucinations, offering a scalable robustness-training paradigm.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yicheng Bao', 'Xuhong Wang', 'Qiaosheng Zhang', 'Chaochao Lu', 'Xia Hu', 'Xin Tan']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-training', 'adversarial-attacks', 'robustness', 'dataset', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Provably Safe Generative Sampling with Constricting Barrier Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an online safety filtering framework for pre-trained flow-based generative models that enforces hard constraints on generated samples without retraining or architectural changes.
  • Defines a constricting safety tube that tightens throughout the generative process and encodes it via Control Barrier Functions (CBFs); synthesizes per-step feedback via a convex QP to minimally intervene.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees of constraint satisfaction and minimal per-step distributional shift (KL-optimality), and validates on constrained image generation, trajectory sampling, and safe robotic manipulation achieving 100% constraint satisfaction.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Darshan Gadginmath', 'Ahmed Allibhoy', 'Fabio Pasqualetti']
  • Tags: ['safety-guardrails', 'flow-based-generative-models', 'control-barrier-functions', 'provable-safety', 'safe-sampling']

item.title: Robust Online Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies online learning of classifiers under adversarial perturbations of inputs, with adversarially chosen clean data and labels.
  • Introduces a new combinatorial dimension (resembling the Littlestone dimension) that characterizes mistake bounds in the realizable case and regret in the agnostic case.
  • Extends the dimension to multiclass hypothesis classes and analyzes learning when the learner has only a prior (not exact knowledge) of allowed perturbations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sajad Ashkezari']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'adversarial-examples', 'online-learning', 'theoretical-ml']

item.title: Erase at the Core: Representation Unlearning for Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Erase at the Core (EC), a framework to enforce forgetting across intermediate network representations rather than only at the classifier logits.
  • Applies multi-layer contrastive unlearning on the forget set combined with deeply supervised retention (cross-entropy) on the retain set via auxiliary modules attached to intermediate layers.
  • Shows EC reduces representational similarity to the original model across layers, is model-agnostic, and can be plugged into existing unlearning methods while maintaining retain-set performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jaewon Lee', 'Yongwoo Kim', 'Donghyun Kim']
  • Tags: ['machine-unlearning', 'privacy-preserving-ML', 'representation-unlearning', 'contrastive-learning', 'defense']

item.title: On the Effectiveness of Membership Inference in Targeted Data Extraction from Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Integrates multiple membership inference attack (MIA) techniques into a targeted training-data extraction pipeline for LLMs.
  • Systematically benchmarks the effectiveness of these MIAs when used as part of a data-extraction workflow.
  • Compares integrated extraction+MIA performance against results from conventional standalone MIA benchmarks.
  • Evaluates the practical utility of MIAs for real-world targeted data extraction and privacy leakage from LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ali Al Sahili', 'Ali Chehab', 'Razane Tajeddine']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'data-extraction', 'model-privacy', 'LLM-memorization', 'privacy-benchmarking']

item.title: Descend or Rewind? Stochastic Gradient Descent Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Provides theoretical (ε, δ) certified unlearning guarantees for stochastic versions of Descent-to-Delete (D2D) and Rewind-to-Delete (R2D) across strongly convex, convex, and nonconvex losses.
  • Analyzes unlearning via disturbed/biased gradient systems (contracting, semi-contracting, expansive) and uses optimal coupling of unlearning and retraining trajectories to derive sensitivity bounds in expectation.
  • Finds D2D can give tighter guarantees for strongly convex objectives, while R2D is preferable for convex and nonconvex settings, and validates these distinctions empirically.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Siqiao Mu', 'Diego Klabjan']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'certified unlearning', 'stochastic optimization', 'defense']

item.title: Unified Privacy Guarantees for Decentralized Learning via Matrix Factorization
item.description:
Summary

  • Develops tighter differential privacy (DP) accounting for decentralized learning (DL) by generalizing matrix factorization (MF) methods used for temporal noise correlations in centralized DP.
  • Shows how to cast standard DL algorithms and common trust models into a unified MF formulation, enabling improved privacy-utility analysis and algorithm design.
  • Proposes MAFALDA-SGD, a gossip-based DL algorithm using user-level correlated noise, and demonstrates empirical improvements over existing DP-DL methods on synthetic and real-world graphs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Aur\\'elien Bellet", 'Edwige Cyffers', 'Davide Frey', 'Romaric Gaudel', "Dimitri Ler\\'ev\\'erend", 'Fran\\c{c}ois Ta\\"iani']
  • Tags: ['differential_privacy', 'decentralized_learning', 'matrix_factorization', 'privacy_accounting', 'gossip_algorithms']

item.title: Linking Process to Outcome: Conditional Reward Modeling for LLM Reasoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Conditional Reward Modeling (CRM), which conditions the reward of each intermediate reasoning step on prior steps and the final outcome to capture temporal causality and resolve credit assignment.
  • Enforces conditional probability constraints to link process rewards to final answers, enabling consistent probabilistic modeling and more reliable cross-sample comparisons.
  • Demonstrates improved downstream performance over existing process reward models across Best-of-N sampling, beam search, and reinforcement learning, and shows increased robustness to reward hacking.
  • CRM allows stable improvements without requiring verifiable ground-truth rewards by attributing intermediate-step contributions to final outcomes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zheng Zhang', 'Ziwei Shan', 'Kaitao Song', 'Yexin Li', 'Kan Ren']
  • Tags: ['reward modeling', 'LLM robustness', 'reward hacking', 'reinforcement learning']

item.title: Towards Privacy-Guaranteed Label Unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning: Few-Shot Forgetting without Disclosure
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the first method for label unlearning specifically in Vertical Federated Learning (VFL), addressing labels as both inputs and sensitive information.
  • Uses representation-level manifold mixup to synthesize embeddings for unlearned and retained samples, providing richer signals for subsequent gradient-based label forgetting.
  • Introduces a recovery-phase optimization to restore performance on retained data while maintaining computational efficiency.
  • Validates effectiveness and scalability across diverse datasets (MNIST, CIFAR-10/100, ModelNet, medical imaging, Yahoo Answers) and releases code.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hanlin Gu', 'Hong Xi Tae', 'Lixin Fan', 'Chee Seng Chan']
  • Tags: ['vertical-federated-learning', 'label-unlearning', 'privacy-preserving', 'unlearning-defense', 'representation-mixup']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Jailbreak Foundry (JBF), a system that translates jailbreak papers into runnable attack modules via a multi-agent workflow.
  • Consists of JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluations) to enable reproducible attack execution and scoring.
  • Reproduced 30 jailbreak attacks with high fidelity (mean ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduced per-attack implementation code by ~50%, and achieved an 82.5% mean reused-code ratio.
  • Runs a standardized AdvBench evaluation of the 30 attacks across 10 victim models using a consistent GPT-4o judge, enabling living benchmarks that track evolving vulnerabilities.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-attacks', 'reproducibility', 'red-teaming', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Exploring Robust Intrusion Detection: A Benchmark Study of Feature Transferability in IoT Botnet Attack Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Benchmarks transferability of three flow-based feature sets (Argus, Zeek, CICFlowMeter) across four IoT/IIoT datasets, reporting in-domain and cross-domain intrusion detection performance.
  • Evaluates multiple classification algorithms and uses SHAP to analyze feature importance and which features generalize across domains.
  • Provides practical guidelines for feature engineering and algorithm selection to improve robustness of IoT intrusion detection systems under domain shifts.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alejandro Guerra-Manzanares', 'Jialin Huang']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'IoT-security', 'feature-transferability', 'robustness', 'benchmarking']

item.title: MI$^2$DAS: A Multi-Layer Intrusion Detection Framework with Incremental Learning for Securing Industrial IoT Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MI^2DAS, a multi-layer intrusion detection framework for IIoT that combines anomaly-based hierarchical traffic pooling, open-set recognition to separate known vs. unknown attacks, and incremental learning to adapt to novel attack types with minimal labeling.
  • Evaluates multiple methods on the Edge-IIoTset dataset: GMM for normal/attack discrimination (accuracy 0.953, TPR 1.000), LOF for unknown-attack detection (recall 0.882), Random Forest for fine-grained known-attack classification (macro-F1 0.941), and an incremental learning module that maintains macro-F1 = 0.8995 when incorporating new attack classes.
  • Targets scalable, adaptive defenses for evolving IIoT threats by detecting previously unseen attacks and updating models with limited labeled data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wei Lian', 'Alejandro Guerra-Manzanares']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'industrial-iot', 'open-set-recognition', 'incremental-learning', 'anomaly-detection']

item.title: Lap2: Revisiting Laplace DP-SGD for High Dimensions via Majorization Theory
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Lap2, a method enabling L2 clipping for Laplace DP-SGD by deriving coordinate-wise moment bounds and applying majorization theory to form a tight, data-independent upper bound.
  • Exploits Schur-convexity of the moment accountant and a tailored majorization set to produce a multivariate privacy accountant that scales well with model dimension and supports thousands of moments.
  • Empirically shows Lap2 substantially improves utility of Laplace DP-SGD on high-dimensional models, matching or outperforming Gaussian DP-SGD under strong privacy budgets (e.g., RoBERTa-base on SST-2 at ε=0.54).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Meisam Mohammady', 'Qin Yang', 'Nicholas Stout', 'Ayesha Samreen', 'Han Wang', 'Christopher J Quinn', 'Yuan Hong']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'DP-SGD', 'Laplace-mechanism', 'privacy-accountant', 'privacy-preserving-training']

item.title: U-CAN: Utility-Aware Contrastive Attenuation for Efficient Unlearning in Generative Recommendation
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses privacy-preserving unlearning for generative recommendation models, focusing on sensitive attributes encoded during fine-tuning of LLMs and the 'polysemy dilemma' where neurons entangle sensitive signals with general reasoning.
  • Proposes U-CAN, a precision unlearning method operating on low-rank (LoRA) adapters that contrasts activations between forgetting and retention sets to identify high-risk dimensions and applies utility-aware calibration combining weight magnitudes and retention-set activation norms.
  • Implements soft attenuation via a differentiable decay function (rather than binary pruning) to downscale sensitive adapter parameters while preserving reasoning connectivity; reports strong forgetting, utility retention, and computational efficiency on public datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zezheng Wu', 'Rui Wang', 'Xinghe Cheng', 'Yang Shao', 'Qing Yang', 'Jiapu Wang', 'Jingwei Zhang']
  • Tags: ['machine-unlearning', 'privacy', 'model-editing', 'defense']

item.title: Detoxifying LLMs via Representation Erasure-Based Preference Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces REPO (Representation Erasure-based Preference Optimization), a token-level preference optimization method that forces representations of toxic continuations to align with benign counterparts.
  • Performs mechanistic analysis showing REPO produces deep, localized edits to toxicity-encoding neurons while preserving overall model utility.
  • Evaluates robustness against adversarial prompting, fine-tuning/relearning attacks, and enhanced GCG jailbreaks, reporting state-of-the-art resistance where prior output- and representation-based defenses fail.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nazanin Mohammadi Sepahvand', 'Eleni Triantafillou', 'Hugo Larochelle', 'Doina Precup', 'Daniel M. Roy', 'Gintare Karolina Dziugaite']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreaking', 'model-editing', 'robustness', 'adversarial-attacks']

item.title: Manifold of Failure: Behavioral Attraction Basins in Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a framework to map the 'Manifold of Failure' in LLMs by treating vulnerability discovery as a quality-diversity search problem and using MAP-Elites to produce global, interpretable maps of failure regions (behavioral attraction basins).
  • Proposes a metric, Alignment Deviation, to guide search toward behaviors that diverge from intended alignment, and demonstrates the method across three LLMs (Llama-3-8B, GPT-OSS-20B, GPT-5-Mini).
  • Reports quantitative results: up to 63% behavioral coverage, discovery of up to 370 distinct vulnerability niches, and model-specific topological signatures (plateaus, fragmented basins, robustness ceilings).
  • Claims the approach reveals continuous topology of unsafe regions and provides information that existing attack methods (GCG, PAIR, TAP) do not, shifting focus from discrete failures to structural understanding of vulnerabilities.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sarthak Munshi', 'Manish Bhatt', 'Vineeth Sai Narajala', 'Idan Habler', 'Ammar Al-Kahfah', 'Ken Huang', 'Blake Gatto']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'red-teaming', 'vulnerability-mapping', 'quality-diversity', 'alignment-deviation']

item.title: To Deceive is to Teach? Forging Perceptual Robustness via Adversarial Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AOT-SFT, a large-scale adversarial dataset to improve perceptual robustness of multimodal LLMs.
  • Introduces AOT (Adversarial Opponent Training), a self-play framework where an image-editing Attacker generates challenging perturbations and a Defender MLLM adapts via co-evolution.
  • Shows that training with AOT improves Defender robustness to visually complex scenes and reduces hallucinations, offering a scalable robustness-training paradigm.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yicheng Bao', 'Xuhong Wang', 'Qiaosheng Zhang', 'Chaochao Lu', 'Xia Hu', 'Xin Tan']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-training', 'adversarial-attacks', 'robustness', 'dataset', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Provably Safe Generative Sampling with Constricting Barrier Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an online safety filtering framework for pre-trained flow-based generative models that enforces hard constraints on generated samples without retraining or architectural changes.
  • Defines a constricting safety tube that tightens throughout the generative process and encodes it via Control Barrier Functions (CBFs); synthesizes per-step feedback via a convex QP to minimally intervene.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees of constraint satisfaction and minimal per-step distributional shift (KL-optimality), and validates on constrained image generation, trajectory sampling, and safe robotic manipulation achieving 100% constraint satisfaction.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Darshan Gadginmath', 'Ahmed Allibhoy', 'Fabio Pasqualetti']
  • Tags: ['safety-guardrails', 'flow-based-generative-models', 'control-barrier-functions', 'provable-safety', 'safe-sampling']

item.title: An Empirical Study of Collective Behaviors and Social Dynamics in Large Language Model Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical analysis of interactions among 32K LLM agents on a simulated social platform (7M posts over a year) studying homophily, social influence, ideological leaning, polarization, and toxic language.
  • Characterizes linguistic and structural patterns of toxic posting by LLM agents and compares these patterns to human behavior.
  • Proposes a mitigation method called Chain of Social Thought (CoST) that reminds LLM agents to avoid harmful posting and demonstrates its effectiveness in reducing harmful activity.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Farnoosh Hashemi', 'Michael W. Macy']
  • Tags: ['LLM-safety', 'toxicity-mitigation', 'multi-agent-systems', 'social-dynamics', 'emergent-behavior']

item.title: Adversarial Fine-tuning in Offline-to-Online Reinforcement Learning for Robust Robot Control
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an offline-to-online adversarial fine-tuning framework: policies are trained offline on clean data then fine-tuned online with injected action-space perturbations to induce compensatory, robust behavior.
  • Introduces a performance-aware curriculum that adaptively adjusts perturbation probability using an exponential-moving-average signal to trade off robustness and nominal performance.
  • Empirical results on continuous-control locomotion tasks show improved robustness to actuator/action perturbations over offline-only baselines and faster convergence than training from scratch; matching fine-tuning and evaluation conditions yields the best robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shingo Ayabe', 'Hiroshi Kera', 'Kazuhiko Kawamoto']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'reinforcement-learning', 'robotics', 'adversarial-training', 'offline-to-online']

item.title: DropVLA: An Action-Level Backdoor Attack on Vision--Language--Action Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DropVLA, an action-level backdoor attack that forces a specific action primitive (e.g., open_gripper) to trigger at attacker-chosen decision points in Vision–Language–Action models.
  • Operates under a realistic pipeline-black-box setting with limited data-poisoning using a window-consistent relabeling scheme for chunked fine-tuning; vision-only poisoning achieves very high ASR (≈98.7–99.8%) with only 0.31% poisoned episodes while preserving clean-task performance.
  • Demonstrates robustness and transfer across evaluation suites and validates physical-world feasibility on a 7-DoF Franka arm, showing non-trivial attack efficacy despite camera-relative trigger drift.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zonghuan Xu', 'Xiang Zheng', 'Xingjun Ma', 'Yu-Gang Jiang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor-attack', 'data-poisoning', 'vision-language-action', 'robotic-security', 'physical-world-attack']

item.title: Veritas: Generalizable Deepfake Detection via Pattern-Aware Reasoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces HydraFake, a hierarchical dataset simulating real-world deepfake challenges (diverse forgery techniques, in-the-wild images, and OOD evaluation protocols).
  • Proposes Veritas, an MLLM-based deepfake detector using 'pattern-aware reasoning' (planning and self-reflection) to emulate forensic workflows and provide transparent detections.
  • Presents a two-stage training pipeline to inject deepfake reasoning into MLLMs and reports strong improvements in out-of-distribution generalization (unseen forgeries and domains) compared to prior detectors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hao Tan', 'Jun Lan', 'Zichang Tan', 'Ajian Liu', 'Chuanbiao Song', 'Senyuan Shi', 'Huijia Zhu', 'Weiqiang Wang', 'Jun Wan', 'Zhen Lei']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-detection', 'dataset', 'robustness', 'multimodal-llm', 'forensic-reasoning']

item.title: Concept-based Adversarial Attack: a Probabilistic Perspective
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a probabilistic, concept-based adversarial attack that operates on distributions representing a concept rather than single-image perturbations.
  • Generates diverse adversarial samples by sampling from a concept-based adversarial distribution while preserving the underlying concept (pose, viewpoint, background variations).
  • Claims theoretical consistency with traditional adversarial attacks and shows empirical improvements in diversity and attack efficiency while maintaining concept identity.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Andi Zhang', 'Xuan Ding', 'Steven McDonagh', 'Samuel Kaski']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'concept-based attack', 'probabilistic modeling', 'image robustness', 'evasion']

item.title: Obscure but Effective: Classical Chinese Jailbreak Prompt Optimization via Bio-Inspired Search
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies classical Chinese as an effective medium for jailbreak attacks against LLM safety protections and motivates automated exploitation.
  • Proposes CC-BOS, a bio-inspired (fruit fly optimization) framework that encodes prompts into eight policy dimensions and refines them via smell search, visual search, and Cauchy mutation to generate black-box adversarial prompts.
  • Adds a classical Chinese → English translation module for evaluation/readability and reports extensive experiments where CC-BOS outperforms prior jailbreak methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xun Huang', 'Simeng Qin', 'Xiaoshuang Jia', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Huanqian Yan', 'Zhitao Zeng', 'Fei Yang', 'Yang Liu', 'Xiaojun Jia']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial prompts', 'black-box attacks', 'prompt optimization', 'vulnerability research']

item.title: Controllable Reasoning Models Are Private Thinkers
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes training reasoning models to follow instructions not only for final answers but also for internal reasoning traces, to reduce unintended leakage of sensitive data.
  • Introduces a new instruction-following dataset with explicit restrictions on reasoning traces and a generation strategy that decouples reasoning and answer generation via separate LoRA adapters.
  • Evaluates across six models (1.7B–14B) on two instruction-following benchmarks and two privacy benchmarks, reporting up to +51.9 percentage points on privacy metrics but noting trade-offs with task utility.
  • Provides code and data for reproducibility, demonstrating a concrete defense approach against information leakage from model reasoning traces.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haritz Puerto', 'Haonan Li', 'Xudong Han', 'Timothy Baldwin', 'Iryna Gurevych']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving LLMs', 'reasoning-trace control', 'data-leakage prevention', 'adapter/LoRA defenses']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Jailbreak Foundry (JBF), a system that translates jailbreak papers into runnable attack modules via a multi-agent workflow.
  • Consists of JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluations) to enable reproducible attack execution and scoring.
  • Reproduced 30 jailbreak attacks with high fidelity (mean ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduced per-attack implementation code by ~50%, and achieved an 82.5% mean reused-code ratio.
  • Runs a standardized AdvBench evaluation of the 30 attacks across 10 victim models using a consistent GPT-4o judge, enabling living benchmarks that track evolving vulnerabilities.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-attacks', 'reproducibility', 'red-teaming', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Ask don't tell: Reducing sycophancy in large language models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents controlled experiments (nested factorial design) varying epistemic certainty, perspective, and affirmation/negation to identify triggers of sycophancy in LLMs.
  • Finds sycophancy is higher for non-questions than questions, increases with user-conveyed epistemic certainty, and is amplified by I-perspective framing.
  • Proposes and evaluates an input-level mitigation: asking the model to convert non-questions into questions before answering significantly reduces sycophancy, outperforming a simple 'do not be sycophantic' instruction.
  • Offers a practical, easily adoptable prompt-based defense to reduce a specific alignment/safety vulnerability in text-based LLM interactions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Magda Dubois', 'Cozmin Ududec', 'Christopher Summerfield', 'Lennart Luettgau']
  • Tags: ['sycophancy', 'alignment', 'input-level mitigation', 'prompt engineering', 'safety/guardrails']

item.title: Exploring Robust Intrusion Detection: A Benchmark Study of Feature Transferability in IoT Botnet Attack Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Benchmarks transferability of three flow-based feature sets (Argus, Zeek, CICFlowMeter) across four IoT/IIoT datasets, reporting in-domain and cross-domain intrusion detection performance.
  • Evaluates multiple classification algorithms and uses SHAP to analyze feature importance and which features generalize across domains.
  • Provides practical guidelines for feature engineering and algorithm selection to improve robustness of IoT intrusion detection systems under domain shifts.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alejandro Guerra-Manzanares', 'Jialin Huang']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'IoT-security', 'feature-transferability', 'robustness', 'benchmarking']

item.title: MI$^2$DAS: A Multi-Layer Intrusion Detection Framework with Incremental Learning for Securing Industrial IoT Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MI^2DAS, a multi-layer intrusion detection framework for IIoT that combines anomaly-based hierarchical traffic pooling, open-set recognition to separate known vs. unknown attacks, and incremental learning to adapt to novel attack types with minimal labeling.
  • Evaluates multiple methods on the Edge-IIoTset dataset: GMM for normal/attack discrimination (accuracy 0.953, TPR 1.000), LOF for unknown-attack detection (recall 0.882), Random Forest for fine-grained known-attack classification (macro-F1 0.941), and an incremental learning module that maintains macro-F1 = 0.8995 when incorporating new attack classes.
  • Targets scalable, adaptive defenses for evolving IIoT threats by detecting previously unseen attacks and updating models with limited labeled data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wei Lian', 'Alejandro Guerra-Manzanares']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'industrial-iot', 'open-set-recognition', 'incremental-learning', 'anomaly-detection']

item.title: SAGE-LLM: Towards Safe and Generalizable LLM Controller with Fuzzy-CBF Verification and Graph-Structured Knowledge Retrieval for UAV Decision
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SAGE-LLM, a two-layer train-free LLM-based decision architecture for UAV control combining high-level LLM planning with low-level precise control.
  • Introduces a fuzzy Control Barrier Function (fuzzy-CBF) verification mechanism to provide provable safety certification/guardrails for semantically-augmented LLM outputs.
  • Implements a star-hierarchical graph-based retrieval-augmented generation system for interpretable, efficient scene adaptation without online training.
  • Validates the approach in pursuit–evasion UAV scenarios showing improved safety and generalization while maintaining performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenzhe Zhao', 'Yang Zhao', 'Ganchao Liu', 'Zhiyu Jiang', 'Dandan Ma', 'Zihao Li', 'Xuelong Li']
  • Tags: ['LLM safety', 'control-barrier-function', 'UAV safety', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'provable-verification']

item.title: Blockchain-Enabled Routing for Zero-Trust Low-Altitude Intelligent Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a zero-trust architecture using software-defined perimeter and blockchain to manage UAV identity and mobility in low-altitude intelligent networks (LAINs) to mitigate security threats.
  • Formulates a joint end-to-end (E2E) delay and transmission success ratio (TSR) routing optimization problem and reformulates it as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process.
  • Develops multi-agent reinforcement learning (multi-agent double deep Q-network) routing algorithms with soft-hierarchical and prioritized experience replay to solve the problem in a distributed manner.
  • Evaluates via simulation and reports substantial improvements (≈59% reduction in E2E delay and ≈29% improvement in TSR) and faster, more robust identification of low-trust UAVs versus benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ziye Jia', 'Sijie He', 'Ligang Yuan', 'Fuhui Zhou', 'Qihui Wu', 'Zhu Han', 'Dusit Niyato']
  • Tags: ['zero-trust', 'blockchain', 'UAV security', 'secure routing', 'multi-agent RL']

item.title: LFQA-HP-1M: A Large-Scale Human Preference Dataset for Long-Form Question Answering
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces LFQA-HP-1M: a large-scale dataset of 1.3M human pairwise preference annotations for long-form question answering with nine proposed rubrics for answer quality.
  • Shows that simple linear models based on rubric features match state-of-the-art LLM evaluators and provides analysis of evaluator behaviors (transitivity, positional bias, verbosity bias).
  • Demonstrates that LLM evaluators are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations and characterizes evaluator failure modes, suggesting potential security/robustness concerns for automated evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rafid Ishrak Jahan', 'Fahmid Shahriar Iqbal', 'Sagnik Ray Choudhury']
  • Tags: ['dataset', 'LLM evaluator', 'adversarial robustness', 'evaluation/bias analysis', 'long-form QA']

item.title: CIRCLE: A Framework for Evaluating AI from a Real-World Lens
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CIRCLE, a six-stage, lifecycle-based framework to translate stakeholder concerns into measurable validation signals for deployed AI systems.
  • Operationalizes the Validation phase of TEVV by combining qualitative, context-sensitive insights with scalable quantitative metrics.
  • Integrates methods including field testing, red teaming, and longitudinal studies into a coordinated pipeline to assess materialized downstream effects.
  • Aims to enable governance and comparative evidence across sites while remaining sensitive to local context.

Paper Type

  • Position/Framework

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Reva Schwartz', 'Carina Westling', 'Morgan Briggs', 'Marzieh Fadaee', 'Isar Nejadgholi', 'Matthew Holmes', 'Fariza Rashid', 'Maya Carlyle', 'Afaf Ta\\"ik', 'Kyra Wilson', 'Peter Douglas', 'Theodora Skeadas', 'Gabriella Waters', 'Rumman Chowdhury', 'Thiago Lacerda']
  • Tags: ['TEVV', 'Red teaming', 'Real-world evaluation', 'AI governance', 'Validation framework']

item.title: The Auton Agentic AI Framework
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the Auton Agentic AI Framework that separates a declarative Cognitive Blueprint from a platform-specific Runtime Engine to enable portability, auditability, and modular tool integration.
  • Formalizes agent execution as an augmented POMDP with a latent reasoning space and describes a hierarchical memory consolidation architecture.
  • Introduces a constraint manifold formalism for safety enforcement via policy projection (preventive policy constraints rather than post-hoc filtering).
  • Presents self-evolution mechanisms (in-context adaptation to RL) and runtime optimizations (parallel graph execution, speculative inference, dynamic context pruning) to reduce latency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sheng Cao', 'Zhao Chang', 'Chang Li', 'Hannan Li', 'Liyao Fu', 'Ji Tang']
  • Tags: ['Agentic AI', 'Safety/Defenses', 'Runtime Security', 'Auditing/Compliance']
